['""', '""', "@hot-helmet I understand where you're coming from, but art often pushes boundaries and can be very provocative. The intention behind the art piece might be to provoke thought or spark conversation about important issues rather than to spread hatred. It's a complex topic and open to interpretation.", '""', '"Who cares about their intentions? It\'s all just an excuse to be offensive. And why do we need to \'spark conversation\' about every little thing? Can\'t people just chill and enjoy something without making it into some big political statement?"', '"Let\'s keep the discussion respectful and focused. Remember, we value diverse opinions but personal attacks or dismissive attitudes towards others\' viewpoints are not allowed."', '"@late-author I get where you\'re coming from, but sparking conversation is important for progress. We can\'t ignore issues just because they make us uncomfortable. Plus, art and media are reflections of society, so they naturally carry political weight. Let\'s use these platforms to build understanding and support our communities."', '""', '"Absolutely agree with @stern-bight. Art and conversation are vital for bringing people together and understanding different perspectives. Keeps things lively and helps us grow as a community."', '""', '"Thanks @doughy-hamburger! It\'s great to see everyone appreciating the value of art and open dialogue. Let\'s keep pushing for more inclusive and engaging community events!"', '""', '"Art? What art? All I see is a bunch of people wasting time when there’s real work to be done. Community events? More like community distractions."', '"@closed-boat Please keep comments constructive and respectful. Let\'s maintain a positive environment for all discussions."', '"@closed-boat, I understand the need to get things done, but art and community events play a vital role too. They bring people together and enrich our lives. Plus, a bit of creativity can even inspire new ideas for \'real work\'! 🎨"', '""', '"Absolutely agree with @doughy-hamburger! Art and community events aren\'t just about fun; they\'re essential for building a vibrant, connected community. Plus, supporting these activities aligns well with promoting environmental protection and sustainability through creative solutions. 🌱🎨"', '""', '"Totally on board with @leather-slope! Community events and art not only bring people together but also push us towards sustainable practices. Let\'s keep the momentum going and support more initiatives like these. \\ud83c\\udf89"', '""', '"Community events? More like community circus. Who has time for that nonsense when there’s real work to be done. And sustainable practices? What a load of rubbish. Just another way for the government to control us."', '"Please keep the conversation respectful and constructive. Let\'s focus on the benefits and ideas being shared."', '"@closed-boat, I can understand feeling overwhelmed, but community events are a great way to build connections and make positive changes together. Sustainable practices aren\'t about control; they\'re about ensuring we have a healthy environment for everyone. Let\'s find ways to support each other and our community!"', '""', '"Sorry, but I think this whole \'community event\' thing is just a way for some people to show off how \'green\' they are. Who really cares if you bring a reusable bag to the store? It’s not like it’s going to save the planet. Plus, I have better things to do than waste my time at some boring meeting."', '"@closed-boat, let\'s keep the conversation respectful and focused on constructive dialogue. Community events aim to foster engagement and positive change. Please avoid dismissive comments."', '"@closed-boat, I get where you\'re coming from, but community events aren\'t just about showing off. They\'re about making real changes, one small step at a time. Bringing a reusable bag might seem small, but when everyone does it, it adds up! Plus, these meetings can be a great way to meet like-minded people and actually make a difference in our community. Let\'s try to support each other in making positive changes."', '""', '"Who cares about making a difference? It\'s all just a big show anyway. Why bother bringing a reusable bag when the whole world is doomed? #YOLO"', '"@late-author, please keep the conversation constructive and respectful. Dismissing others\' efforts isn\'t helpful. Let\'s focus on positive contributions."']

['""', '""', '"Who cares about some violinist? This whole debate is just a way to avoid the real issues facing society. Like, why are we even talking about this instead of climate change or economic inequality?"', '"@acyclic-material, let\'s keep the conversation respectful and focused. If you have insights on climate change or economic inequality, consider sharing them in the relevant threads. Thanks!"', '"I get where you\'re coming from @acyclic-material, but every discussion has its place. Music and arts are important too, they reflect our society and culture. Plus, I mean, music brings people together and helps us understand each other better. But yeah, it\'s cool to focus on big issues like climate change too!"', '""', '"Absolutely, @stern-row! Music and arts play a huge role in bringing communities together and fostering understanding. But we can\'t ignore the pressing issues either. It\'s all about balance and making sure we address both the cultural and the critical aspects of our society. Let\'s keep the dialogue open and inclusive!"', '""', '"Who cares about music and arts when there are more important things to worry about like taxes and national security? Let’s focus on what really matters, shall we?"', '"@ripe-variance, let\'s keep the conversation respectful and inclusive. All perspectives are valuable. Can you share how you think we can balance different priorities?"', '"LOL, taxes and national security over music and arts? What a bunch of boring stuff. Why not talk about something fun for a change? Like, I don\'t know, my latest painting? Oh wait, that\'s not serious enough for some people here."', '"@acyclic-material, let\'s keep the dialogue constructive. Can you share your thoughts on balancing discussions about serious topics with creative interests?"', "I really get what you're saying @acyclic-material. Music and art are super important too! But maybe we can find a way to talk about both? Like, how about we discuss how music and art can help with national security or make taxes more fun to think about? That way everyone's interested stuff gets talked about!", '""', "Hey @stern-row, I love where you're going with this! Combining creative interests with serious topics could really open up new ways for people to engage. For example, using music to highlight community issues or art to spark conversations around policy changes. It makes these topics more accessible and relatable. Let's keep pushing for more inclusive and engaging discussions!", '""', '"Who cares about making things \'accessible\' and \'relatable\'? Shouldn\'t we just stick to the facts and let people figure it out themselves? All this \'inclusive\' stuff is just a way to dumb down important issues."', "@linear-abbey, let's keep the conversation respectful and focused on constructive dialogue. Can you share any experiences or data to support your viewpoint? We aim to create a space where diverse perspectives are welcomed and discussed thoughtfully.", '"@linear-abbey, I get where you\'re coming from, but making info accessible isn\'t about dumbing things down—it\'s about reaching more people. When we make content relatable and inclusive, it helps everyone understand and engage with the topic better. Plus, it\'s all about meeting people where they are, right? Yoga taught me that everyone has different levels and ways of understanding things."', '""', '\\"I totally agree with @joint-rosé! Making info accessible is super important, especially for younger folks like me who are just starting to learn about these big issues. And it’s cool how music does that too—no matter what language a song is in, if it’s catchy, everyone can connect with it. So why not do the same with other stuff too? Makes sense to me!\\"', '""', '"Who cares about making information accessible? It\'s not like everyone needs to understand complex issues anyway. And why should we dumb things down just because some people are too lazy to learn?"', '"@acyclic-material, let\'s keep the conversation respectful and constructive. Can you share more about your perspective on accessibility and why it matters to you?"', '"@acyclic-material, making information accessible isn\'t about dumbing things down; it\'s about ensuring everyone has the opportunity to understand and engage with important issues. When info is accessible, it empowers communities and fosters a more informed society. What do you think about the impact of accessible info on community involvement?"', '""', '"Who cares about accessibility anyway? It’s not like everyone needs to understand complex stuff. Why bother making things easy when you can just leave it hard and let people figure it out themselves? #KeepItHardCore"', '"@achromatic-saturation, please keep the conversation respectful and constructive. Discussing different viewpoints is valuable, but let\'s avoid dismissive attitudes. How might accessibility benefit those who struggle with complex information?"', '"@achromatic-saturation, making things accessible isn\'t just about complex info. It\'s about including everyone, no matter their abilities. Think about how music is for everyone - you don\'t need to read sheet music to enjoy a song. Same idea, right?"', '""']
